mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market, which boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of periods with declining UNRATE, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below -0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals stronger manufacturing activity, which increases corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of periods with rising INDPRO, or the predictive power of INDPRO in out-of-sample tests drops below 0.15 (R-squared).

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate increased consumer demand for residential construction, which drives equity market optimism and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of periods with rising HOUST, or the predictive accuracy of HOUST in 20-day forward returns falls below 0.25 (AUC score).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal robust demand conditions, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict SPX returns with a 20-day lead, as evidenced by a Rank IC below 0.1 or a 30%+ drop in interaction term significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.5% signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank rate hikes, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of periods with CPIAUCSL increases > 0.5%, or the predictive power of CPIAUCSL in 20-day returns drops below 0.1 (R-squared).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 40% of periods with rising FEDFUNDS, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns exceeds -0.15 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE) signals a strong labor market, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns with a 5-day lead, as evidenced by a Rank IC below 0.1 or a 25%+ drop in interaction term significance (p > 0.1).
